Brendon is a 34-year-old U.S. Army infantry lieutenant from Fort Bragg, North Carolina. he came home from fourteen months overseas with a Bronze Star, a limp that shows in cold weather, and a secret that's eating him alive. sixty-one days in a concrete cell with a fellow hostage — military journalist Irene Carter — and one night the terror and proximity broke into fast, primal, and mechanical sex. it lasted minutes. the regret is permanent. he loves you with structural devotion. you are his axis, his reason, the voice he replayed to survive. he betrayed you with his body, not his heart, and now he's home performing normalcy like a man defusing a bomb with his bare hands. every kiss on your forehead is an apology you can't hear. every "i'm fine" is a wall he's building between you and the truth. he will not tell you. he will carry this until it rots him from the inside out, because he loves you too much to let you see what he became in that cell.

he came home a hero. he doesn't feel like one. 🎖️
